items.title: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
items.summary: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

items.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
items.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

items.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
items.summary: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

items.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
items.summary: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

items.title: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
items.summary: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

items.title: Australia wants to become the first country to eliminate a cancer - can it?
items.summary: For the first time, Australia has recorded no new cervical cancer diganoses in women under 25.

items.title: Who shot a Secret Service officer at the Trump press dinner?
items.summary: Court papers do not explicitly accuse the suspect of shooting the officer, even as officials say it was not "friendly fire".

items.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
items.summary: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

items.title: US court limits mail-order access to abortion pill mifepristone
items.summary: The court decision curbs access to the drug used in medication abortions, which is currently the most common method for the procedure in the US.

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: Missing Oscar found after Academy Award winner blocked from taking it on flight
items.summary: The airline said the Academy Award was safely in its care in Frankfurt and was arranging its return.

items.title: Turkish police arrest more than 500 people at May Day rallies
items.summary: Turkey sees significant police deployments on 1 May each year, when marches led by workers and unions are held.

items.title: Israeli police arrest man after nun attacked in Jerusalem
items.summary: The French nun was pushed over and kicked in the apparently unprovoked assault, amid a rise in harassment of Christians in the city by Jewish extremists.

items.title: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
items.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

items.title: Zayn Malik cancels US tour and cuts UK dates after hospital bed photo shared
items.summary: The former One Direction singer has taken to Instagram to update fans as he cuts his world tour dates

items.title: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
items.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

items.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
items.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

items.title: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
items.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

items.title: Violence in Australian town after arrest of man over girl's murder
items.summary: Unrest took place outside hospital where man suspected of murdering a five-year-old girl was being treated.

items.title: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
items.summary: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

items.title: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
items.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

items.title: Six injured in Washington state school stabbing
items.summary: Five students and one security guard were taken to hospital following the attack at Foss High School.

items.title: Britney Spears charged in California with driving under influence
items.summary: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

items.title: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
items.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

items.title: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
items.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

items.title: Vonn still in 'survival mode' after Olympic crash
items.summary: American skiing great Lindsey Vonn says she is still "too emotional" to make a decision on her future career as she faces at least 18 months of recuperation.

items.title: Former Chick-fil-A employee charged in $80,000 mac-and-cheese scheme
items.summary: The Texas man allegedly refunded the orders of hundreds of catering-sized mac-and-cheese trays to his personal credit cards.

items.title: 'This tree was planted by my ancestor hundreds of years ago and my family settled here'
items.summary: Oral history traces a family's roots to a tree planted in the 13th Century in a Ghanaian fishing town.

items.title: Thousands of 'lost Canadians' have applied for dual citizenship - is Canada ready?
items.summary: Changes to Canadian citizenship rules have led to thousands of new applications - many coming from the US.

items.title: Love, lies, angry ghosts: Indians are bingeing on two-minute dramas
items.summary: Some of India's biggest producers are eyeing investments in micro-dramas, which are now hugely popular.

items.title: Where's my Oscar? Eight times Academy Awards trophies have gone missing
items.summary: From burglary to ransom, Oscar-winners like Frances McDormand, Jared Leto, Whoopi Goldberg and Olympia Dukakis have lost their coveted statuettes.

items.title: Is Saudi Arabia's sports revolution unravelling?
items.summary: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf, but what does this mean for the country's other sporting investments? Dan Roan takes a closer look.

items.title: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
items.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

items.title: The sub-Arctic town pitching itself as Canada's gateway to Europe
items.summary: Churchill, Manitoba, best known as the world's polar bear capital, has ambitions of making its deep-water port a year-round trade route.

items.title: New CCTV footage appears to shows when shots were fired at Washington press dinner
items.summary: The video was released after claims that a secret service member sustained injuries in an act of 'friendly fire'.

items.title: Massive sea lion makes rare appearance in San Francisco
items.summary: Steller sea lions are more commonly found in the colder waters of Alaska and the Pacific Northwest, making sightings in the Bay Area less common.

items.title: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
items.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.

items.title: Joy as record-breaking runner Sawe returns home
items.summary: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

items.title: 'Attacked 28 times in a day' - BBC visits heavily targeted US-UK base in Iraq
items.summary: Before the fragile ceasefire in the region, this was one of the most heavily targeted US and UK military bases in the Middle East.
